name: Chaport Live Chat API
what_it_does: Live chat platform with REST API — enabling businesses to add a customer support chat widget to their website, manage conversations, auto-invite visitors to chat, sync customer data, and build chat automation, with JavaScript API for widget customization and REST API for data access and integration with CRM and helpdesk systems.
use_cases: Adding live chat widget via Chaport JavaScript API for website customer support
Retrieving chat conversations via Chaport REST API for CRM integration and sync
Pre-populating visitor data via Chaport API for personalized chat experiences
Automating chat invitations via Chaport for proactive customer engagement triggers
Building chatbots via Chaport Bot API for automated first-response support
not_for: Enterprise helpdesk with complex ticketing workflows (use Zendesk or Freshdesk)
AI-first customer support automation (use Intercom or Drift for advanced AI chat)
Large enterprise with SLA requirements (Chaport is SMB-focused)
best_when: A small to mid-size business needs a simple, affordable live chat widget with basic API access — where Chaport provides competitive pricing with enough API capability for basic CRM integration.
avoid_when: You need enterprise helpdesk workflows, advanced AI chat automation, or complex multi-channel customer support.

auth.notes: API key for REST API. JavaScript widget API for front-end integration. Webhooks for chat events. No OAuth2. No fine-grained scopes. API key from Chaport account settings. Bot API for automated responses. Separate webhook token for signature validation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan: unlimited agents, unlimited chats (limited features)
pricing.notes: Russian company. Founded 2015. Bootstrapped. Tawk.to and Chaport are among cheapest live chat options. Free plan is generous. Used by: small businesses, agencies, freelancers. Bot API for basic automated responses. Auto-invitation rules. Visitor analytics. File sharing in chat. Mobile apps (iOS/Android). Multi-language (45+ languages). Note: Russian company — data residency considerations for EU customers.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API with API key. JS widget API. Webhooks. Russian company. GDPR claimed. Limited public API documentation. Free plan available. SMB-focused.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Conversation reads idempotent by ID. Visitor data reads idempotent. Message sends not idempotent — check for existing message before sending.
known_agent_gotchas: Russian company — data may be hosted in Russia; check GDPR compliance for EU customer chat data
Limited REST API — primarily event-driven via webhooks for chat data
Bot API for automated responses — separate from REST API; different integration pattern
Free plan available but API features may be limited to paid plans
Less mature API than Intercom/Zendesk — documentation may have gaps

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR claimed. Russian company — data residency concern for EU compliance. API key auth. No OAuth2 granularity. Customer chat data (PII conversations) — verify data residency before deploying for EU users. Limited security documentation publicly available.
